questions.question: Global economic shifts, political instability, and rapid technological change can function as external pressures that influence employees’ job performance.
The spillover perspective proposes that emotions, attitudes, and behaviours developed in one domain (work or family) can transfer into the other domain.
Self-reliance refers to the incapacity to depend on one’s own judgement and abilities, which gradually develops through repeated practice and real-life experience.
A situation where expectations from one role make it difficult to fulfil expectations from another role is known as _________________.
Which of the following outcomes generally reflects a positive rather than a negative impact of stress in organizations?
A non-profit organization develops community spaces that promote health awareness and hygiene education. This initiative mainly strengthens which resilience strategy?
Successful time management depends not only on planning alone, but also on consistently practising behaviours that support the completion of goal-oriented tasks. Is this statement correct?

Individuals who manage their time efficiently often experience which of the following outcomes?
A. Higher perceived job performance
B. Greater satisfaction with work and personal life
C. Clearer understanding of role expectations
D. Increased somatic stress symptoms
options: Only A and B are correct
Only A, B and C are correct
All of the above are correct

questions.question: The natural preference individuals have for being more active and productive at particular times of the day is referred to as _________________.
When stress levels are moderate rather than extreme, individuals are typically _________________.

Assertion (A): Remaining silent about unfair workplace behaviour reflects strong emotional intelligence.
Reason (R): Emotional intelligence involves self-awareness, responsible behaviour, and constructive communication.
options: Both A and R are true, and R explains A
Both A and R are true, but R does not explain A
A is false, but R is true
A is true, but R is false

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about resilience:
A. It helps individuals continue functioning effectively during challenging situations
B. It allows individuals to recover after facing setbacks
C. It supports the maintenance of overall well-being
options: Only A and B are correct
Only A and C are correct
All A, B, and C are correct

questions.question: Which of the following responses is least effective for dealing with stress in the workplace?
options: Taking brief breaks to reset during demanding tasks
Avoiding discussion of stress and hoping it resolves on its own
Seeking advice from experienced colleagues or mentors
Trying to identify and manage the root causes of stress

questions.question:
Organizations aiming to support employees’ work-life balance may introduce which of the following policies?
A. Flexible scheduling options
B. Parental or family leave provisions
C. On-site childcare services
D. Alternative work arrangements, such as remote work
options: Only A, B, and C are correct
Only B, C, and D are correct
All A, B, C, and D are correct

questions.question: Meera works long hours in consulting but also prioritizes painting and spending weekends with family. Which approach best represents a healthy work–life balance strategy?
options: Keeping strict boundaries between professional and personal roles
Shifting attention effectively between work responsibilities and personal interests
Seeking satisfaction from both professional achievements and personal activities
Combining all of the above approaches
